role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; co-subject of a joint oral history with her husband Emmett W. Bassett (catalog entry 38).
ai_reading: Priscilla Tietjen Bassett shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 38 (catalog slug 'emmett-w-bassett-and-priscilla-tietjen-bassett'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Priscilla Tietjen Bassett is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 38 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Priscilla Tietjen Bassett; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Priscilla Tietjen Bassett's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 38
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Crowd gathered around the Reflecting Pool at the 1963 March on Washington
gallery.caption: The crowd at the 1963 March on Washington, the event Priscilla Tietjen Bassett and her husband Emmett describe attending in their oral history.
gallery.photographer: U.S. Information Agency, Press and Publications Service
